topic: RW-002 integration verification — Section 6.10 Steelman Evidence Ladder, 4 forward-reference -> inline-preview substitutions
change_summary: RW-002 (sloppytoppy Phase 2 rewrite) integrated into data/sections.json. Four bare "Section X.Y" forward-references in Section 6.10 substituted with inline-preview sentences citing the target section anchor. Integration matches rewritten_text verbatim except for 4 anchor refinements made by the decider (#part2/4/6 -> specific section IDs).
our_argument_summary: Section 6.10 (Steelman Evidence Ladder) engages the dome's self-organized ranking of its strongest claims and shows each tier reduces to a known failure mode (calibration-as-prediction, relabeled standard physics, circular derivation from globe constants, fitted-constant-tested-against-fit-data). Verdict and claim unchanged by this rewrite — RW-002 was a forward-reference rewrite, not an argumentative refactor.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — section-level rewrite verification, no dome code claims at issue.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review, not a WIN — code_analysis tags are documented here only because the schema requires them. The section discusses the dome's prediction registry and OTS methodology but does not itself make a code claim that would carry a monitoring tag.

integration_check.diff_summary: Integrated content differs from RW-002.rewritten_text in exactly four locations, all anchor-refinement adjustments made by the decider during integration:

anchor_refinements.verdict: improvement: links to the specific Section 2.9 details block rather than the Part 2 tab anchor

anchor_refinements.location: STEELMAN-03 Tesla — parenthetical after find_curve.py reference
STEELMAN-03 Tesla — closing sentence after tautology argument
anchor_refinements.verdict: improvement: links to specific Section 6.2 details block rather than Part 6 tab anchor
improvement: links to specific Section 4.1 details block rather than Part 4 tab anchor

p6-62-1.note: sole inbound reference is this Section 6.10 preview — new

integration_check.content_preservation_verdict: PASS — all 75 distinct numeric/identifier tokens from RW-002.CONTENT_PRESERVATION_AUDIT.numbers_preserved are present in the integrated text. Citations (Chapman 1933, US 787412) preserved. Verdict and claim language unchanged.

preview_text_accuracy.preview: which shows the V13 Finsler coordinate system is self-referential because its input coordinates are derived from globe distances
preview_text_accuracy.target_tldr_match: ACCURATE — Section 2.9 TLDR states the formula is circular and that input coordinates are derived from globe distances. "Self-referential" is a defensible paraphrase of "circular: converts globe positions to dome coordinates, computes a predicted distance, and compares it against the globe distance it started from."

preview_text_accuracy.preview: which documents the circular conversion of globe positions into dome coordinates
preview_text_accuracy.target_tldr_match: ACCURATE — direct paraphrase of Section 2.9 TLDR phrase "converts globe positions to dome coordinates."

preview_text_accuracy.preview: which shows the dome's 40,030 km disc diameter equals 2π × R_Earth to within 170 meters because the disc radius is really a spherical arc length
preview_text_accuracy.target_tldr_match: ACCURATE — Section 6.2 TLDR opens with the same 40,030 km / 2πR_Earth / 170-meter claim verbatim and includes the spherical-arc-length explanation.

preview_text_accuracy.preview: which audits the dome's 28-scored / 39-domain / 61.46σ Fisher-combined dashboard and shows the count keeps growing without any new physics
preview_text_accuracy.target_tldr_match: ACCURATE — Section 4.1 TLDR opens with the 61.46σ / 28-scored / 39-domain figures and the "the count has kept growing without any new physics" phrase appears verbatim.

kernel_of_truth.description: The forward-reference substitution genuinely improves the surface for a flat-earth-level reader (monitor/sloppytoppy/rewrites/RW-002.json:rationale). Bare "Section 2.9" / "Section 4.1" references force the reader to jump tabs and search for relevant material; the inline previews let them decide whether to follow the link without leaving the steelman analysis (data/sections.json:part6.html). The decider's anchor refinement (#part2 -> #2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop) is a defensible polish over the rewriter's output and lands the reader on the exact details block rather than the parent tab (verified via id-attribute counts in data/sections.json).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Integration-verification reviews do not bear on the substantive verdict — Section 6.10's argument that each Steelman tier reduces to a documented failure mode is unchanged (data/sections.json:part6.html, the four "Why it fails" paragraphs are byte-identical except for anchors). This review confirms readability improved without argumentative content drift; the verdicts on each Steelman tier remain as before (STEELMAN-01: calibration-as-prediction; STEELMAN-03: globe-derived Tesla tautology; STEELMAN-04: λ_g fitted-constant tautology).

text_fingerprint.delta_explanation: +100 chars from longer anchor IDs in 4 substituted hrefs (~25 chars each on average)

deeper_analysis: RW-002 was the validation case for PROP-041 Category G (forward-reference resolution) — the surface had previously been PUNTed (PUNT-002, 2026-05-16T09:33Z) because Categories A/B/C/D/E could not address forward_references (monitor/sloppytoppy/rewrites/RW-002.json:rewrite_category_rationale). Category G was added in amendment-002 specifically to handle bare-section-reference substitution with one-sentence inline previews drawn from target TLDRs (monitor/sloppytoppy/rewrites/RW-002.json:preview_source_refs). The four substitutions in this RW are the first production-mode test of that category, and they pass the substantive criteria: (1) preview text faithfully summarizes the target TLDR (4/4 verified against actual TLDR text), (2) anchor IDs resolve to existing details blocks (4/4 resolved), (3) no content drift in the surface itself (numbers, claims, citations preserved per the RW's own audit and reconfirmed here). The two "Part 5" / "Part 5, Test 1" bare references left unsubstituted are correctly out-of-scope per audit-script VALID_KINDS (rewrite rationale notes this gap and routes it to operator for amendment-003 consideration).

citation_check.note: No new external citations introduced by RW-002 — Chapman (1933) and US 787412 patent were already present in original_text and preserved (per CONTENT_PRESERVATION_AUDIT.citations_preserved). No DOI verification scope for this integration check.

summary_for_decider: PASS — RW-002 integration is clean. Content-preservation audit holds (75/75 numbers, 1/1 citation, verdict/claim unchanged). All 4 inline previews accurately reflect target-section TLDRs (Section 2.9 ×2, Section 6.2 ×1, Section 4.1 ×1). Anchor refinement done by decider during integration (#part2/4/6 -> specific section IDs) is an improvement over rewriter output. ZERO holes. Recommendation: pop queue_id 376 and mark RW-002 integration verified. Minor observation for tinker, not for this review: the rewriter (sloppytoppy-rewrite) emitted generic tab-level anchors in rewritten_text href values (#part2, #part4, #part6) rather than the specific section IDs implied by preview_source_refs[].target_id ("2.9", "6.2", "4.1"). The decider correctly resolved these during integration. If Phase 2 RW volume scales, surfacing this rewriter discipline gap to tinker (rewriter prompt or audit-script enforcement of href specificity) would save the decider from anchor-cleanup work per RW. Not blocking.
